steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student asserts that escaped transgenic fish “may have significant negative long-term ecological effects.” To support this, we need a quotation that explains how the escapes cause ecological harm — a specific mechanism linking the fish to damage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "In one site in the wild where transgenic fish were observed, females outnumbered males, while in another the numbers of females and males were equivalent.": Observes a sex-ratio difference but explains no harm from it. No ecological damage mechanism. ✗
  • Choice B — "Though some presence of transgenic fish in the wild has been recorded, there are insufficient studies of the impact of those fish on the ecosystems into which they are introduced.": Says we don't yet know the impact — the opposite of supporting a claim that harm exists. ✗
  • Choice C — "The ecosystems into which transgenic fish are known to have been introduced may represent a subset of the ecosystems into which the fish have actually been introduced.": Suggests the problem is wider than known, but still gives no mechanism of harm. ✗
  • Choice D — "Through interbreeding, transgenic fish might introduce the trait of fluorescence into wild fish populations, making those populations more vulnerable to predators.": Provides a direct causal pathway: interbreeding spreads fluorescence $\Rightarrow$ wild fish become more visible to predators $\Rightarrow$ ecological damage. This is the harm mechanism the assertion requires. ✓
That matches Choice D — "Through interbreeding, transgenic fish might introduce the trait of fluorescence into wild fish populations, making those populations more vulnerable to predators.".
